message:
feat(wal): add segment recovery for parallel WAL (D1.7)

Implements recovery infrastructure for replaying WAL records from
multiple segment files after a crash. Segments are read in epoch
order and records are applied to reconstruct the database state.

Key additions:
- SegmentRecoveryResult struct with recovery statistics
- SegmentRecoveryOptions for controlling recovery behavior
- recover_segments() reads all segments and returns records in order
- recover_and_apply_segments() applies records to a page cache HashMap
- max_durable_epoch() finds the highest epoch from existing segments
- cleanup_segments() removes all segment files (post-checkpoint)

The recovery path:
1. List all segment files for the database
2. Sort by epoch (ascending)
3. Read and deserialize records from each segment
4. Apply after_images in epoch order (later epochs overwrite earlier)
5. Optionally delete segments after successful recovery

Tests added:
- Basic recovery with 3 segments
- Recovery and apply to page cache with delete-after-recovery
- Max durable epoch tracking
- Cleanup segments

This completes the parallel WAL infrastructure. Pager integration
(D1.3-D1.4) is blocked by D2 work.

Epic: bd-3wop3 (Eliminate 8-16 Thread Contention)
Bead: bd-3wop3.1 (Per-thread WAL buffers + epoch group commit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
